articles.title: Gas Prices Fall as Iran Talks Buoy Markets
articles.description: The national average price of regular gasoline fell nearly 17 cents over the past week.

articles.title: Older drivers hit with new tax charges in 2026 for petrol, diesel and EV cars
articles.description: Vehicle Excise Duty rates rose from April for all UK motorists

articles.title: Transport association urges government to cut fuel prices as crude falls to $90/barrel
articles.description: The All India Motor & Goods Transport Association is calling on the Central Government to lower petrol and diesel prices. They point to a significant drop in international crude oil rates. The association states that consumers and transporters are not benefiting from this global price fall. Higher fuel costs are impacting essential goods and contributing to inflation.

articles.title: Did petrol, diesel prices rise on May 30? Check latest fuel rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Hyderabad
articles.description: The prices have remained constant after the last hike on May 25, which was the fourth straight hike this month. | India News

articles.title: High gas prices are just the start - inflation is seeping into the rest of the economy
articles.description: Energy shocks tied to Middle East turmoil are now feeding into housing, travel and everyday essentials, leaving Kevin Warsh’s Fed with a tough choice.

articles.title: Wait for Iran deal continues after Trump says he’ll make ‘final determination’
articles.description: President Donald Trump is yet to make a decision over a deal that could extend the current ceasefire with Iran, with no announcement on Friday following a Situation Room meeting in which he said he would make a “final determination.”Oil prices have dipped this week amid the persistent reports that Trump and Iran could be close to sealing the agreement that has eluded the two sides for over a month

articles.title: Crude oil prices decline to six‑week low over tentative US‑Iran deal
articles.description: US West Texas Intermediate (July delivery) settled down 1.73 per cent at USD 87.36 a barrel, while international oil benchmark Brent crude (August contract) fell 1.7 per cent to close at USD 91.12 this week.

articles.title: Tiny Guyana poised for big Iran oil gains and growth strains
articles.description: Guyana was already the world’s fastest growing economy before the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran drove up oil prices. Now, the tiny Caribbean nation of nearly 1 million people will reap an even bigger bonanza as the conflict reshapes global energy markets.
